content.problemStatement:
Om has an array AAA of size NNN and an integer BBB.

He wants to find whether there exists a **non-empty** subsequence of AAA such that the bitwise AND of all elements in the subsequence is equal to BBB.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and BBB — the size of array and the target integer, respectively.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers, the ithi^{th}ith integer representing the element at the ithi^{th}ith index.




Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` if any such subsequence exists and `NO` otherwise.


You may print each character in uppercase or lowercase. For example `YES`, `Yes`, `yes`, and `yES` are all considered the same.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 100000
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and B — the size of the array and the target value. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing "YES" if there exists a non‑empty subsequence whose bitwise AND equals B, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: Elements 7 (111) and 13 (1101) both contain bits of 5 (101). Their AND is 7 & 13 = 5, so answer YES.\nTest case 2: No element contains both bits of 2, therefore any subsequence fails.
Element 3 itself already satisfies (3 & 3) == 3 and the AND of the single‑element subsequence {3} equals 3.
Bit 2 (value 4) of B is not present in any array element, so no subsequence can produce AND 7.

hidden.label: tricky case where extra bits cannot be cleared

solution.approach: For the AND of a subsequence to be exactly B, every chosen element must contain all bits that are set in B, i.e., (x & B) == B. Consider only those elements. Let cur be the bitwise AND of all such elements. If at least one element satisfies the condition and cur equals B, we can pick the whole set and obtain AND = B. Otherwise it is impossible. The algorithm scans the array once, counts qualifying elements and maintains cur using a 30‑bit mask.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = list(map(int, sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()))
    it = iter(data)
    T = next(it)
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = next(it); B = next(it)
        cnt = 0
        cur = (1 << 30) - 1
        for _ in range(N):
            x = next(it)
            if (x & B) == B:
                cnt += 1
                cur &= x
        out_lines.append('YES' if cnt > 0 and cur == B else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
